project.name: Tugas VAR
project.currentFocus: WebAR AR Coffee Menu cup object readability on the Hiro marker
durableFacts: The project is a static HTML/CSS/JavaScript WebAR demo using A-Frame and AR.js from ar.html.
AR scene geometry is owned by ar.html; menu state, rotation, marker status, and HUD interactions are owned by script.js.
The coffee cup object should remain small, marker-anchored, and readable as a cup rather than a flat brown circle.

lastAchievements: Added minimal project and design docs required by the local governance hard gate.
Reworked the AR coffee object into a small tapered cup with rim, dark surface, handle, saucer, and subtle steam.
Kept Rotate, Next, and Info wiring in script.js while preserving the side-view cup tilt.
pendingIssues: Manual camera validation with the Hiro marker is still required on a browser/device.
nextBestActions: Open http://127.0.0.1:5500/ar.html from the local static server or deploy over HTTPS and scan the Hiro marker.
Verify that Next keeps all menu variants around scale 0.45 and that Rotate spins without losing the -15 degree tilt.
validationState: git diff --check passed with only existing CRLF normalization warnings.
Local static server responded 200 for /ar.html on port 5500.

designContinuity.currentConceptualAnchor: hand-thrown ceramic demitasse on a cafe saucer
